key_insight: GUMP-Net combines object detection, anatomy-aware edge learning, and deep level-set evolution to deliver interpretable multi-class pelvic segmentation.
engineering_relevance: It supports more reliable pelvic fracture planning and navigation by improving segmentation accuracy and robustness, especially when labeled data are limited.
method: Automatic object detection initializes the level set.
An edge detector learns anatomy-aware boundary cues.
An iteration module performs deep level-set evolution for segmentation refinement.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive experiments on pelvic datasets and additional tests on an ankle dataset, with improved accuracy, robustness, and consistency versus state-of-the-art methods, especially in small-data settings.
watch_item: Performance and interpretability may depend on quality of initialization and boundary learning, so generalization should be checked carefully across anatomies and imaging protocols.
paper_title: GUMP-Net: An interpretable model-data-driven intelligent algorithm for multi-class pelvic segmentation
